Change lanes to the right and slow to merge because construction barriers close/shift the left side and a motorcycle is already in the right‑curving work‑zone lane, requiring a safe gap during the merge and following distance.
Lane change to the right due to construction blocking the current lane ahead and merge-right signs, then match speed to follow the slow motorcycle after merging.
Merge right and slow to tuck in behind the motorcycle due to a construction zone closing the left side and narrowing traffic into a single right lane, requiring a safe gap and reduced speed.
Nudge right due to construction barriers blocking the left side of our lane
Lane lane change to the right due to construction blocking the current lane ahead and merge-right guidance, then match speed behind the slow motorcycle after merging.

Lane speed up to complete the right merge into the single open lane because construction barriers and a slow scooter ahead block/ending our current path, requiring us to take the right curve and line up behind it; maintain a safe following gap as our lane narrows, then prepare for merge back as traffic flow allows it after we clear the construction zone.
Lane at reduced speed to merge right because of construction blocking our lane ahead
Lane a right merge due to construction narrowing/closing the left side and a slow motorcycle ahead, then match its speed after merging into the right lane.

Lane change and merge to the right due to construction barriers narrowing/closing the left side and a merge-right sign, then slow to safely slot in behind a slow two-wheeler ahead on a right-curving, low-speed construction zone.
Lane change since the left lane is closed by construction ahead
Lane change for the right curve because construction barriers block straight ahead
Lane change to our right due to construction blocking our current lane ahead and merge-right signs, then match speed to follow the slow motorcycle after merging.

Lane change to follow the rightward merge through the construction zone and position behind/around the slow motorcycle ahead, then reduce speed due to constrained space and a slow lead vehicle in front after merging right.
Lane change to maintain traffic flow because of a slow motorcycle ahead

Lane change to it to merge right because construction barriers close the left lane ahead, slowing to merge safely behind/around the motorcycle already in that lane, then continuing with gentle acceleration after the merge is completed; later prepare to merge back left as space narrows near construction equipment and signage ahead, but not immediately after this action is taken.
Lane change to the motorcycle due to construction blocking the current lane ahead and merge-right guidance, then match speed to follow the lead motorcycle through the narrowed work-zone section.

Lane change to the motorcyclist and slow down due to a construction work zone narrowing/closing lanes ahead, merging into the right channel behind the motorcycle and car to maintain safe following distance and clearance to the right curb.

Lane change to the right scooter due to a construction work-zone closing/redirecting the left side and to merge behind the slow motorcycle, then match its speed for safe following distance.

Lane change to the right vehicle lane due to construction barriers blocking the left lane ahead, merging into the right-lane traffic and slowing for slower vehicles (scooter and cars) after merging, then accelerating once established in the right lane.
Lane change to the right motorcyclist due to left-lane closure from construction and a slow scooter ahead, then match speed with right-lane traffic after merging.
Lane change to the right cyclist ahead blocking the current lane

Lane change to the right due because construction barriers block the current lane ahead and merge-right signs indicate a rightward detour; the merge is executed with a slight speed reduction to slot in behind a motorcycle already in that lane, then the lane narrows and becomes constrained by construction equipment and cones ahead, requiring further slowing after the merge.
Lane change to the right due ahead construction blocking the current lane and merge-right signs, then slow to merge behind the motorcycle in the narrowed work-zone corridor.
Lane change to the right due in a construction zone that shifts traffic right and a slow lead motorcycle, then reduce speed to merge safely into the right-lane queue behind it.

Lane change to the right due as the left lane is closed by construction and a merge-right sign, merging into the right lane behind slower motorcycles; then reduce speed to maintain a safe following gap after the merge.
Lane change to the right due to we-lane closure from construction ahead and to merge safely behind the motorcycle in the open right lane, then match its slower speed after merging.
Lane change to the right due to a construction work zone blocking the left lane ahead, merging into the right-lane traffic and slowing to maintain a safe gap behind the motorcycle.
Lane change to the right due to the left lane being closed by construction barriers ahead, merging into the right-lane traffic and slowing to maintain a safe gap behind the motorcycle.

Lane change to the right due to our lane ending at a construction barrier ahead and merge-right signage, merging into the right lane behind slower motorcycles; then reduce speed to maintain a safe following gap after the merge.
Lane change to the right due to construction controls blocking the current lane ahead and merge-right guidance, then match speed behind the slow motorcycle after merging.
Lane change to the right due to construction merges narrowing/closing the left side ahead, and slow slightly to merge behind the motorcycle already in that lane.
